subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Article IV, WJP, RSF, Transparency International) + Australian official (ABS, RBA, APRA, Treasury) + Australian independent press (ABC, SMH, The Conversation).
Source-lean: HIGH Anglophone (Australia is Anglophone); China-leverage and AUKUS-delivery risks may be under-weighted given the thin non-Anglophone read.
Climate (bushfire/flood/drought) and insurance affordability are the standout physical drags.

economic.notes: Low public debt and a credible central bank, with revenue and trade leaning on commodity exports to a single dominant buyer.

fiscal_state.notes: Low public debt by advanced-economy standards; a modest deficit after two surpluses; revenue leans heavily on iron-ore exports (~70% to China), the key swing risk. (IMF 2025 Art IV; Treasury MYEFO)

monetary_independence.notes: The RBA is credible and independent, actively defending its 2-3% target with a new Monetary Policy Board. (RBA)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The AUD is a freely floating own currency and a minor global reserve currency (~2% of reserves) - a modest benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: A near-term flare (headline ~4.8% peak mid-2026, above band) easing back to ~2.5% by 2028 under RBA tightening; housing costs structural. (RBA SMP May 2026)

banking_stability.notes: A concentrated, well-capitalised big-four; sound lending; tail risk from very high household debt and housing-affordability stress. (RBA FSR Mar 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A commodity exporter with stable, rules-based policy, but heavy China concentration in iron ore and a latent paradox-of-plenty (revenue tracks one buyer/one commodity) caps the long view. (Dept of Industry)

institutional.notes: Strong rule of law and independent statistics; press freedom dragged by hyper-concentrated ownership.

rule_of_law.notes: Top-tier (WJP ~11/142), an independent judiciary, and an operating federal anti-corruption commission. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: The ABS is independent and highly regarded, with no documented interference. (ABS)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A competent, professional public service with effective delivery (Medicare, migration). (observed)

press_freedom.notes: Free but structurally narrow: no constitutional press guarantee and among the developed world's most concentrated media ownership. (RSF 2025; ACMA)

political_social.notes: A durable democracy with a lawful but tightening migration system; cohesion strained by the immigration/housing debate and unresolved Indigenous reconciliation.

political_stability.notes: Labor re-elected in a May 2025 landslide; durable institutions and peaceful transfers. (Australian Electoral Commission)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong protections; no bill of rights and some surveillance/anti-encryption laws are watch-items. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A lawful, durable skilled-points/employer system (~70% skilled), but a deliberate net-migration cut makes it markedly more selective; an offshore-detention legacy is a negative. (Dept of Home Affairs)

social_cohesion.notes: Generally high but at cyclical lows; the 2023 Voice referendum left Indigenous reconciliation unresolved, and migration/housing sharpened debate. (Scanlon)

demographics.notes: Below-replacement fertility and ageing; immigration-fed growth now deliberately throttled, weakening the long-term offset. (ABS)

geopolitical.notes: Deep alliances with front-line Indo-Pacific exposure to China tension.

alliance_reliability.notes: Deep ANZUS/Five Eyes and a 50-year AUKUS treaty, though the submarine pathway is partly dependent on US/UK delivery. (UK Commons Library)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active wars, but front-line Indo-Pacific exposure to China tension. (ASPI; CSIS)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Follows Western sanctions, not a major imposer; an open capital account, no controls. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: Universal healthcare and low crime, against severe and worsening climate exposure and eroding insurance affordability.

climate.notes: Black Summer bushfires, Murray-Darling drought, and Great Barrier Reef mass-bleaching (5 events since 2016); wealthy/high-readiness but severe heat/fire exposure worsening. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Universal Medicare with strong outcomes and a 2025-26 bulk-billing expansion; GP-access pressures remain. (Services Australia; AMA)

infrastructure.notes: Strong in major cities; a housing-supply shortfall is the key strain. (budget; observed)

crime_safety.notes: Low violent crime and homicide; firearm violence very low post-1996. (AIC context)

disaster_insurance.notes: Premiums up ~51% in five years, ~15% of households in affordability stress, and flood-cover gaps; functional but eroding. (Actuaries Institute 2026; APRA)

summary: A durable democracy with strong rule of law, low public debt, a credible central bank, universal healthcare, and low crime. The drags are heavy economic concentration in commodity exports to a single dominant buyer, hyper-concentrated media ownership, a deliberately tightening (if lawful) migration system, and severe, worsening climate exposure with eroding insurance affordability. Front-line Indo-Pacific geography adds latent geopolitical risk.
